reachability_fact:
symbolic_regression.iii:1157-1177 (`symreg_make_in_commit`): `SYMREG_HASH_SCRATCH` is 8192 bytes (line 144). Max dataset = MAX_POINTS*(MAX_VARS+1)*9 = 256*9*9 = 20736 bytes. `symreg_fold_dataset` (line 1183) has a `if (w + 9u64) <= cap` guard that silently drops rows once `w >= 8183`. For any request with >907 points*vars the in-commit is a truncated prefix, not a full binding of the dataset — violating M10/M12. Caller path: `symreg_regress` (line 1263) → `symreg_emit_witness` (line 1094) → `symreg_make_in_commit`. The KAT exercises only 3 points (corpus_631.iii line 4 → symreg_selftest → kat_identity/affine/twovar, each 3 rows × 1-2 vars = 54-81 bytes), so the truncation path is live but not exercised by existing KATs.

synthesis_spec.iii: All G1-G9 fixes are verified applied in the live file. `cons_find` sentinel check at line 573 is `if cfound == SYNSPEC_CONS_SENT` (correct). `cad_begin(1u32)/payload/final` (lines 468-500) is Keccak-256 (cad.iii:40 `CAD_SUITE_KECCAK256 : u32 = 1u32`), byte-identical to `keccak256_oneshot` (verified by cad_selftest KAT). KAT 2 expected length 148 is arithmetic-correct. Bounds guards at lines 284-285 and 309-310 are present. No real defects remain in synthesis_spec.iii.
maximal_design:
Replace the staged-copy + cad_oneshot path in `symreg_make_in_commit` with a streaming `cad_begin(1u32)` / `cad_payload(sig_base, sig_len)` / per-row `cad_payload` / `cad_final` sequence. This eliminates the 8192-byte cap entirely — the keccak streaming state handles arbitrarily large inputs. No scratch buffer needed for staging (the 8192-byte `SYMREG_HASH_SCRATCH` remains as a module-global but is only used by `symreg_make_out_commit` for the 32-byte `ident_from_bytes` result; the size can be reduced to 32 bytes).

The streaming design also removes the `symreg_fold_row` / `symreg_fold_dataset` helpers (they existed only to copy bytes into the staging buffer) — replacing them with direct `cad_payload` calls over the decoded mirror arrays per row. This is strictly better: no allocation of a large scratch buffer, no silent data truncation, full M10 commitment to the complete dataset for any legal request size.

`synthesis_spec.iii` is CORRECT as written. No changes needed there.

No ABI change: `symreg_make_in_commit` is a private helper (not @export), the public API signatures are unchanged, and the hash output is the same 32-byte Keccak-256 digest — just over the correct (uncapped) input.

code_changes.new_code:
/* in-commit = Keccak256(signature_blob || dataset mirror bytes).
 * Streaming via cad_begin/cad_payload/cad_final (suite 1 = Keccak-256)
 * so no staging buffer cap applies -- full dataset is committed regardless
 * of size.  M10/M12: the commit binds the exact request. */
fn symreg_make_in_commit() -> i32 {
    let sbase : u64 = SYMREG_REQ_SIG
    let slen : u64 = (SYMREG_REQ_SIGLEN as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64
    let outc : *u8 = &SYMREG_IN_COMMIT as u64 as *u8
    cad_begin(1u32)
    let sp : *u8 = sbase as *u8
    if slen > 0u64 { cad_payload(sbase, slen) }
    symreg_stream_dataset()
    cad_final(outc)
    return SYMREG_OK
}

/* Stream the decoded dataset mirror directly to the active cad session.
 * One cad_payload call per row value (sign byte then magnitude 8 bytes),
 * no scratch buffer needed -- keccak streaming handles arbitrary length. */
fn symreg_stream_dataset() -> i32 {
    let npts : u32 = SYMREG_N_POINTS
    let nvar : u32 = SYMREG_N_VARS
    let mut row : u32 = 0u32
    while row < npts {
        symreg_stream_row(row, nvar)
        row = row + 1u32
    }
    return SYMREG_OK
}

/* Stream one dataset row: nvar x-values then the y-value, each as
 * sign:u8 (1 byte) then mag:u64 LE (8 bytes) via cad_payload. */
var SYMREG_STREAM_VAL : [u8; 9]   /* 1 sign + 8 mag bytes, module-scope (Trap 7) */

fn symreg_stream_row(row: u32, nvar: u32) -> i32 {
    let vbase : u64 = &SYMREG_STREAM_VAL as u64
    let vp : *u8 = vbase as *u8
    let rowx : u32 = row * SYMREG_MAX_VARS
    let mut v : u32 = 0u32
    while v < nvar {
        let idx : u64 = ((rowx + v) as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64
        vp[0u64] = SYMREG_DX_SIGN[idx] & 1u8
        symreg_store_u64_le(vbase, 1u64, SYMREG_DX_MAG[idx])
        cad_payload(vbase, 9u64)
        v = v + 1u32
    }
    let ridx : u64 = (row as u64) & 0xFFFFFFFFu64
    vp[0u64] = SYMREG_DY_SIGN[ridx] & 1u8
    symreg_store_u64_le(vbase, 1u64, SYMREG_DY_MAG[ridx])
    cad_payload(vbase, 9u64)
    return SYMREG_OK
}
